frames.title: Decision Logic Is Fragmented
content: Organizations make decisions every day. However, the logic behind those decisions is often scattered across:
As a result, decisions become difficult to understand, govern, test, and improve.

current_problems.project_response: AI Decision Engine: turns decision logic from an implicit process into an explicit system.

frames.title: Decision Execution Is Not Enough
content: Making decisions is only the first step. Organizations must also determine:
Context + Policies + Goals → Next Best Action
and continuously learn from the outcomes.

future_problems.project_response: The long-term objective: a decision system capable of planning, executing, evaluating, and improving decisions over time.

current_capabilities.title: Decision Context
current_capabilities.description: Define the information required for a decision.

current_capabilities.title: Decision Planning
current_capabilities.description: Derive execution order from dependency relationships.
supports: Target Entity → Dependency Graph → Execution Plan

current_capabilities.title: Decision Traceability
current_capabilities.description: Make decision behavior observable and explainable.
includes: Enables debugging and governance of decision systems.

future_capabilities.title: Policy Governance
future_capabilities.description: Support policy ownership, lifecycle management, approval, and versioning.

future_capabilities.title: Decision Learning
future_capabilities.description: Turn decision outcomes into reusable knowledge.

future_capabilities.title: Decision Coordination
future_capabilities.description: Coordinate decisions across multiple contexts, policies and domains.

architectural_goal.toward: Context + Memory + Policies + Goals → Next Best Action
architectural_goal.purpose: The architecture is designed to make decision making observable, testable, and evolvable.

static_structure.title: Context Layer
responsibilities: define the information required for decisions

static_structure.title: Execution Layer
responsibilities: execute decision workflows and produce entities

static_structure.title: Policy Layer
responsibilities: evaluate business rules and generate decisions

static_structure.title: Traceability Layer
responsibilities: make decision behavior observable and explainable

story_groups.title: Core Discovery
story_groups.core_discovery_content: The architecture evolved by progressively moving decisions out of runtime.

stories.title: Dependency Discovery — Plan Before Execute
stories.problem: Before: Execution path did not exist before runtime. while loop: - discover dependency - insert step. Consequences: Pipeline Black Box; execution paths could not be reviewed before execution; dependency issues could only be discovered during execution.
stories.discovery: Planning and execution are different concerns. Planning answers: What should happen? Execution answers: Make it happen.
stories.outcome: After: Dependency Graph → Planner → Execution Plan. Runtime consumed an execution plan, instead of constructing one. Benefits: catch dependency issues before runtime; review execution paths before runtime.

stories.title: Dependency Correctness — Validate Before Execute
stories.problem: Before: Dependency Graph existed, correctness was still checked during execution. Examples: cycle detection; missing producers. Consequences: graph issues were discovered only after execution began.
stories.discovery: Validation is not execution. Planning answers: What should run? Validation answers: Can it run safely? Execution should: assume correctness. Validation should: establish correctness.
stories.outcome: After: Validator. Benefits: detect graph issues before execution.

stories.title: Dependency Definition — Derive Before Duplicate
stories.problem: Before: Dependency definitions existed in multiple places. Examples: Tool Signature; Tool Registry; Entity Mapping. Consequences: the same information required multiple updates; dependency definitions could drift out of sync.
stories.discovery: Tool signatures already described: inputs; outputs. Represent information once. Derive everything else.
stories.outcome: After: Tool Signature → Derived Dependencies. Benefits: dependency definitions exist in one place; graph relationships no longer require manual synchronization.

stories.title: Layer Ownership — Separate Policy From Execution
stories.problem: Before: Business rules lived inside tools. Consequences: policy changes required tool changes.
stories.discovery: Facts answer: What is true? Policies answer: What should happen? Facts and decisions evolve at different rates. They require different ownership boundaries.
stories.outcome: After: Policy Layer. Benefits: policy changes no longer require tool changes.
